provenance.author: Poly by Google
provenance.notes: Coconut palm from Poly by Google, distributed via poly.pizza/m/bXUTyfiwqBb under CC-BY (source labels it 'CC-BY'; normalized to CC-BY-4.0 here, attribution preserved). Normalized via @gltf-transform: centered to a ground-center pivot (x/z symmetric, min.y=0) and webp-compressed (2.38MB -> 329KB). Modeled fronds (not alpha planes), so the baked card silhouette is the true palm shape. Distinct kebab id 'coconut-palm' so it never collides with the legacy camelCase 'coconut' billboard namespace. Credit in THIRD-PARTY-ASSETS.md.

lod.label: mesh-near+octa-far
lod.rationale: A 27m coconut palm is a sparse coastal landmark with a curved asymmetric trunk. Up close it reads as a real fronded crown on a bare trunk (cheap 2.8k-tri mesh); past ~50m it collapses to an 8x3 octahedral impostor so ground-level views remain one coherent palm instead of the previous crossed front-card trunk split. Its narrowed azimuth blend band avoids smearing two adjacent trunk captures across most angles. The far band keeps the previous 140m outer range to avoid expanding residency cost.

bands.notes: 8x3 upper-hemisphere octahedral atlas baked from the normalized mesh via scripts/bake-vegetation-impostor.mjs; batched and finite-ranged at 140m.
